chunks.text: EECS Login Servers | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar EECS Login Servers Windows Remote Desktop IRIS maintains a Windows Remote Desktop Server, winterm.eecs.berkeley.edu , which is a part of the EECS Active

.berkeley.edu , which is a part of the EECS Active Directory infrastructure. Access is via Microsoft Remote Desktop, and is available to all EECS Standard Account holders. Some standard software is installed and available on winterm.eecs (e.g. Office

stalled and available on winterm.eecs (e.g. Office, Visio, Firefox, Chrome, Thunderbird, Notepad++), and your EECS home directory is easily accessible. As of March 21, 2022, access to winterm.eecs from is restricted to campus networks. For off-campus

m is restricted to campus networks. For off-campus use, we recommend using the campus VPN first, and then connecting to winterm.eecs via Microsoft Remote Desktop. Alternatively, you may use the campus Remote Desktop Gateway service to connect securel

Remote Desktop Gateway service to connect securely to winterm.eecs. Linux SSH Servers IRIS maintains a Linux login server, login.eecs.berkeley.edu , which automounts EECS home directories and project spaces. Access is via SSH, and is available to al

spaces. Access is via SSH, and is available to all EECS Standard Account holders. Authentication uses one’s EECS username and EECS Windows/LDAP password. SSH tunneling is not enabled on login.eecs, and running of CPU-intensive applications is not pe

nd running of CPU-intensive applications is not permitted on this shared resource. As of December 19, 2022, access to login.eecs from off-campus is restricted . We recommend using the campus VPN first, and then connecting to login.eecs via SSH. IRIS

, and then connecting to login.eecs via SSH. IRIS also maintains login-mfa.eecs.berkeley.edu , a Linux login server that does allow SSH from off-campus (campus VPN not required). To access it, you will need to enable both an SSH public/private keypai

l need to enable both an SSH public/private keypair and a Time-based One Time Password (TOTP) (e.g. Google Authenticator) secret key. It is similar to login.eecs in all other aspects. See our separate page on Using login-mfa.eecs.berkeley.edu . Prima

page on Using login-mfa.eecs.berkeley.edu . Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
